description: SKILL.md §7 Quality Targets (line 428) claimed 'manual testing package has 18 scenarios across 7 categories' but §3 (line 122), the README, and the on-disk playbook show 32 scenarios across 9 categories (DAC-001..DAC-032). Stale from before council-graph categories 08 (8 scenarios) + 09 (6 scenarios) were added: 18+8+6=32, 7+2=9.

feature_catalog/ has 9 category folders + 32 per-feature files but no root FEATURE_CATALOG.md, which feature_catalog_creation.md §3 makes an invariant ('root file is always FEATURE_CATALOG.md'). The skill was deliberately built playbook-centric: per-feature §4 SOURCE METADATA points to manual_testing_playbook.md as 'Canonical catalog source', and the playbook root §17 carries a FEATURE CATALOG CROSS-REFERENCE INDEX. Authoring a root catalog vs accepting the playbook-centric design is a design decision.
recommended_fix: Author feature_catalog/FEATURE_CATALOG.md per feature_catalog_creation.md §4 (frontmatter, H1, TOC, §1 OVERVIEW, 9 category sections with per-feature summaries + links), OR formally accept the playbook-centric design and document the exception. RECOMMEND authoring the root catalog for 100% conformance.
deferral_rationale: Operator approved authoring (gate decision answered early: '1: author'). feature_catalog/FEATURE_CATALOG.md authored per feature_catalog_creation.md §4 (frontmatter, unnumbered TOC, §1 OVERVIEW, 9 category sections, 32 per-feature capability summaries + links); HVR-clean; all 32 links resolve.

description: scoring_rubric.md line 58 used 'seamless' (HVR §6 extended hard-blocker, replace with 'smooth') as the Integration-dimension top-score anchor.
recommended_fix: Replace '15=seamless' with '15=no friction' (pairs cleanly with '5=minor friction').

description: SKILL.md §7 SUCCESS CRITERIA carries extraction/rename-era milestone framing: a 'Council Skill Completion Checklist' of rename-completion criteria, line 434 'validate.sh --strict accepts the Phase 001 spec folder' (explicit phase reference), and line 435 'Old kebab-case reference links are absent from live callers' (migration check). Per the no-phase-references convention SKILL.md should state present-tense skill logic; phase/migration refs belong in changelog.
recommended_fix: Rewrite §7 SUCCESS CRITERIA to present-tense skill-completion criteria; drop the 'Phase 001 spec folder' + kebab-case migration references. Fold into the Phase-3 SKILL version-bump touch-up or a follow-on.
deferral_rationale: Resolved in Phase 3 during the SKILL version bump: removed the explicit 'Phase 001 spec folder' phase reference (the clear no-phase-refs violation) -> 'The skills packet spec docs pass validate.sh --strict'. The remaining §7 'Council Skill Completion Checklist' + kebab-case-absence criteria are retained as defensible present-tense correctness criteria (they assert the skills desired end state, not phase/test history).

description: README §4 STRUCTURE changelog tree lists only v1.0.0.0/v1.1.0.0; §10 RELATED DOCUMENTS changelog line describes only v1.0/v1.1 ('v1.0.0.0.md skill extraction, v1.1.0.0.md graph...'). Actual changelog head is v2.0.0.0 (5 versions on disk). Stale user-facing listing.
recommended_fix: Correct in the Phase-3 full README rewrite: changelog list + STRUCTURE tree current to v2.x plus the new v2.1.0.0.
deferral_rationale: Resolved in Phase 3: README.md fully rewritten. STRUCTURE tree changelog line + RELATED DOCUMENTS now state 'v1.0.0.0 through v2.1.0.0' (current head), replacing the stale v1.0/v1.1-only listing.

description: All 32 per-feature files' §4 SOURCE METADATA list 'Canonical catalog source: manual_testing_playbook.md'. If a root FEATURE_CATALOG.md is authored (AF-0002), these should point to it instead. Contingent on the AF-0002 design decision.
evidence: feature_catalog/**/*.md §4 SOURCE METADATA (32 files share the pattern)
recommended_fix: If AF-0002 resolves by authoring FEATURE_CATALOG.md, update the 32 'Canonical catalog source' lines to point to it (mechanical cascade). If the playbook-centric design is accepted, no change.
deferral_rationale: AF-0002 resolved by authoring FEATURE_CATALOG.md, so the back-link cascade was applied: all 32 per-feature 'Canonical catalog source' lines updated from manual_testing_playbook.md to FEATURE_CATALOG.md via uniform sed (verified 32/32, 0 leftover). Completes bidirectional cross-linking per feature_catalog_creation.md §7.

description: HVR hard-blocker grep flagged 'holistic' x9 (seat_diversity_patterns + scoring_rubric) and 'harness' x2 (changelog v1.1.0.0). Verified as FALSE POSITIVES: every 'holistic' is the proper-noun 'Holistic Seat' (a defined council lens alongside Analytical/Creative/Critical/Pragmatic/Research seats, not the banned metaphorical 'holistic'); 'harness' is 'test harness' (literal technical noun, not the banned 'harness the power' metaphor). No HVR violation; no edit applied.
recommended_fix: None. Documents the false-positive screen (per verify-audit-agent-P0-claims discipline): grep candidates verified against actual context as legitimate domain terms.
deferral_rationale: Grep candidates verified as legitimate domain terms (Holistic Seat proper noun; test harness literal); no HVR violation, no edit.

description: README §7 RUNTIME PARITY (lines 206-208) and §10 (lines 266-268) reference agent mirror paths '.opencode/agents/deep-ai-council.md', '.claude/agents/deep-ai-council.md', '.codex/agents/deep-ai-council.toml' that DO NOT EXIST. The actual agent files are named 'ai-council.*' (pre-rename name) across FOUR runtimes (.opencode/.claude/.codex/.gemini); their internal identity references deep-ai-council but the files were never renamed. README also omits the .gemini/agents/ai-council.md mirror (claims 3 runtimes, 4 exist).
recommended_fix: IN SCOPE (Phase 3 README rewrite): correct §7/§10 to the actual 'ai-council.*' paths and add the .gemini mirror row. GATE DECISION: the rename-incompleteness (agent files still named ai-council) is cross-cutting outside this packet (renaming risks live '--agent ai-council' callers); recommend a follow-on packet if a full rename is wanted.
deferral_rationale: Resolved in Phase 3 per operator decision ('2: correct, thats fine'): README rewrite folds runtime mirrors into §4 STRUCTURE as a 4-row table pointing to the actual ai-council.* files (OpenCode/.claude/.codex/.gemini), adding the previously-omitted Gemini row. Agent-file RENAME DECLINED (files keep ai-council.* to avoid breaking live --agent ai-council callers); recommended as a separate follow-on packet if a full rename is ever wanted.

description: SKILL.md leads with '## 1. OPERATIONAL MODES' before '## 2. WHEN TO USE', shifting all numbered sections +1 vs skill_md_template (which prescribes WHEN TO USE as §1, SMART ROUTING §2, HOW IT WORKS §3, RULES §4, REFERENCES §5). Surfaced during Phase-4 per-artifact template-match scoring.
recommended_fix: Optional: demote OPERATIONAL MODES to an unnumbered overview block after the H1/tagline (template permits a capabilities overview there) or fold into §4 HOW IT WORKS, then renumber. NOT applied: content is load-bearing (one-CLI-per-round invariant referenced by §5 ALWAYS rule 6), sk-doc package validator passes, and renumbering 8 sections + the §5->§1 cross-ref is churn disproportionate to a cosmetic ordering preference.
deferral_rationale: Accepted documented deviation per the Phase-4 gate criterion ('100% conformance OR explicit deviation log'). Load-bearing + package-valid; recommended as an optional follow-on if strict section-numbering conformance is later required.
